regulation_change_events.change_summary: The European Banking Authority has revised reporting requirements and mandates regarding ICT incident management for Tier 1 financial entities, including a reduced reporting window, automated detection systems, indemnification clauses in vendor contracts, and quarterly risk attestation.
regulation_change_events.regulation_text:
AMENDMENT NOTICE — DORA Article 17 Revised (effective 1 June 2026)
The European Banking Authority has issued a binding revision to the Digital Operational Resilience Act (EU) 2022/2554.

KEY CHANGES:
1. ICT incident reporting window reduced from 24 hours to 4 hours for major incidents
2. Automated detection systems required for all Tier 1 financial entities by 31 December 2026
3. Vendor contracts must include indemnification clauses for ICT outages exceeding 4 hours
4. Board-level ICT risk attestation now required quarterly (previously annual)
5. New requirement: real-time threat intelligence sharing with regulatory sandbox

FULL TEXT: Financial entities must report all major ICT-related incidents to the competent authority within four (4) hours of classification, replacing the previous twenty-four (24) hour window. Major incidents are defined as those meeting two or more of the following criteria: (a) impact on more than 50,000 users; (b) service downtime exceeding 30 minutes; (c) data integrity breach affecting customer records. Automated detection systems capable of classifying incidents within one hour must be deployed by 31 December 2026. Third-party ICT service provider contracts must include indemnification provisions for qualifying outages. The management body must quarterly attest to the effectiveness of ICT risk management controls and submit attestation evidence to the competent authority.

propagation_result.summary: The European Banking Authority has revised reporting requirements and mandates regarding ICT incident management for Tier 1 financial entities, including a reduced reporting window, automated detection systems, indemnification clauses in vendor contracts, and quarterly risk attestation.

propagations.obligation_description: Report all major ICT-related incidents to the competent authority within four hours of classification.
propagations.relevance_reason: Unified Hackathon Fintech, as a neobank, must comply with incident reporting timelines.

propagations.relevance_reason: To manage ICT incidents effectively, the company must employ automated detection systems.

action_plan.task: Procure and install automated detection solutions by deadline.

propagations.obligation_description: Include indemnification clauses in vendor contracts for ICT outages exceeding four hours.
propagations.relevance_reason: Vendor management is crucial, particularly with the reliance on AWS.
